[TASK] explain
[SNIPPET]
Finding: deposit() uses naive conversion that can over/under-mint due to rounding
response: Using raw integer division for shares = assets * totalSupply / totalAssets can round in the wrong direction. This may mint excess shares to early depositors or strand dust. Use ERC4626’s convert/preview helpers with explicit rounding.

[TASK] rewrite_fix
[SNIPPET]
function deposit(uint256 assets) external { uint256 shares = assets * totalSupply / IERC20(asset).balanceOf(address(this)); _mint(msg.sender, shares); IERC20(asset).transferFrom(msg.sender,address(this),assets); }
response: function deposit(uint256 assets) external nonReentrant { uint256 supply = totalSupply; uint256 prev = totalAssets(); IERC20(asset).transferFrom(msg.sender,address(this),assets); uint256 curr = totalAssets(); uint256 actual = curr - prev; uint256 shares = supply==0 ? actual : (actual * supply) / prev; _mint(msg.sender, shares); }
